programmes.show_description: In the first leg of an epic 1300 miles across Nigeria, Michael is in bustling Lagos and an extraordinary waterfront slum school, then dodges alligators at the Emir of Kano's palace
Judge Rinder rules the courtroom in his hit series, but now the respected barrister with a criminal defence background steps out from behind the bench to take on real cases.
This channel is currently off air.
Members of the local constabulary patrol the town of Ashfordly and the nearby village of Aidensfield, keeping the peace whilst also dealing with local loveable rogues.
Cassie, Abigail and Joy browse an estate sale, where Joy hopes to find a clock for Martha's renovation. Stephanie's ex-husband, Wes, comes to town to pack up their shared cabin.
From the planets most stunning state of the art structures to its mightiest modern machines, none would have been possible without the pioneering engineers of the past.
Ainsley is joined by chefs Genevieve and Nico in the kitchen garden at Tyntesfield, Somerset for an al fresco cook off.
Jeremy Wade searches for the real creature behind the myth of the Loch Ness Monster, examining the evidence, reviewing famous sightings and talking to eyewitnesses.
Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home makeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes.
A Left Wing politician causes problems for Yorkshire village Bobby Nick Rowan.
Awe-inspiring, ambitious, and with an innovative, interlinked story arc that marks a first in natural history programming, this landmark series, narrated by Stephen Fry.
Alex Polizzi's continues on her quest to turn badly run hotels into profitable ventures.
Promising news. Rhona expresses her gratitude but is spotted by Lydia. Laurel confides in her friend Nicola.
Todd's friends rally round to support him, Adam and Jodie's feuding turns dirty, Leanne consults Daniel about Sam, and Ollie chastises Maggie for meddling in his affairs.
Sister Brigid tries to convince her superior at the convent to seek medical help. Frankie comes to a decision about Staff Nurse Taylor.
Faced with the death of a police informer on an estate riven with racial tension, Frost finds his patience pushed to the limit.
A love story between a gypsy and the vicar's daughter unfolds and causes trouble in Aidensfield and problems for Nick and Kate.
Holly has finally managed to figure out the motive for the first murder but her personal life is still in turmoil.
Jim's old army mates join Steve at his funeral, Adam's act of revenge puts Jodie on edge, Theo finds a new target, and Megan's love life intrigues Leanne.
Graham gives Rhona a last chance. Arthur rejects Jai's offer of a job. Nicola is suspicious.
Nash and Grady cross paths with Nash's former foster parents, which triggers Nash's long-harbored vendetta towards them, particularly their little boy.
As Stan and Rajib make a hasty retreat across the desert, Marga reasserts her commitment to the führer. Meanwhile, David is in peril in the skies over France.
Judge Rinder rules the courtroom in his hit series, but now the respected barrister with a criminal defence background steps out from behind the bench to take on real cases.

Members of the local constabulary patrol the town of Ashfordly and the nearby village of Aidensfield, keeping the peace whilst also dealing with local loveable rogues.
Martha fills in as the local Dear Debbie advice columnist, getting an anonymous letter that adds intrigue during a progressive dinner attended by all of our Middleton couples.
From the planets most stunning state of the art structures to its mightiest modern machines, none would have been possible without the pioneering engineers of the past.
Ainsley is joined by chefs Ben Ebbrell and Cyrus Todiwala in the kitchen garden of Blicking Hall in Norfolk for a light-hearted cooking contest.
Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade attempts to identify the animal that reportedly dragged stricken passengers from a riverboat that capsized in the Amazon in 1981.

Jim's old army mates join Steve at his funeral, Adam's act of revenge puts Jodie on edge, Theo finds a new target, and Megan's love life intrigues Leanne.
Nurse Taylor's happiness about her future is shattered, Matron is shocked to see a familiar face on the ward and Dr Goodwin tries to put the rumours about himself to rest.
There is plenty on Frost's plate when senior citizens are targeted in a string of vicious attacks, but at least he can call on his attractive new female sergeant for help.
Aidensfield becomes the scene of a murder hunt when a retired top London copper is shot dead.
Kirsten's investigation takes her into the heart of Scottish politics, but when she unearths a Navy cover-up, it leaves Amy questioning who she can trust.
Maggie frets over Ben's hospital appointment, a defamatory article upsets George, Alya meets with her former boss, and Hope urges Sam to stand up to his bully.
Timing is everything. Paddy gets better news. Gabby gives Arthur a new strategy.
Helen Mirren returns again as Detective Superintendent Jane Tennison. Now transferred to the Greater Manchester Police, her reputation for fierce assertiveness precedes her.
